items.definition_en: Fear is the feeling you get when you think something bad might happen.
items.example_en: She felt fear when she heard a noise at night.

items.definition_en: Anger is the strong feeling you have when something makes you upset.
items.example_en: His face was red with anger.

items.definition_en: Sadness is the feeling you have when you are unhappy.
items.example_en: There was sadness in her eyes.

items.definition_en: Worry is the feeling you have when you think something bad will happen.
items.example_en: He felt worry about the test.

items.definition_en: Hate is a strong feeling of not liking someone or something.
items.example_en: There is no place for hate here.

items.definition_en: Stress is the feeling of being worried or unable to relax.
items.example_en: She gets stress from too much work.

items.definition_en: Disgust is a strong feeling of dislike or sickening.
items.example_en: He showed his disgust at the smell.

items.definition_en: Shame is the bad feeling you have when you've done something wrong.
items.example_en: She felt shame about her mistake.

items.definition_en: Boredom is the feeling you get when nothing interests you.
items.example_en: He sighed with boredom during the long meeting.

items.definition_en: Loneliness is the sad feeling of being alone.
items.example_en: She tried to fight her loneliness.

items.definition_en: Regret is the feeling of being sorry about something you did.
items.example_en: He had deep regret after yelling.

items.definition_en: Pain is the feeling you have when something hurts you.
items.example_en: He was in pain after the fall.

items.definition_en: Jealousy is the unhappy feeling you have when you want what someone else has.
items.example_en: Her jealousy caused many arguments.

items.definition_en: Guilt is the bad feeling you have after doing something wrong.
items.example_en: He felt guilt for breaking the vase.

items.definition_en: Shock is the strong feeling you have after something very surprising or bad happens.
items.example_en: He could not speak because of shock.

items.definition_en: Disappointment is the sad feeling when something is not as good as you hoped.
items.example_en: Her face was full of disappointment.

items.definition_en: Envy is the feeling of wanting what another person has.
items.example_en: He felt envy when he saw the new car.

items.definition_en: Frustration is the feeling you get when you cannot do something you want.
items.example_en: She hit the table in frustration.

items.definition_en: Anxiety is the feeling of being very worried about something that might happen.
items.example_en: She felt anxiety before the exam.

items.definition_en: Resentment is the angry feeling you have when you think something is not fair.
items.example_en: He had resentment toward his boss.
